origin.name: South Korea
origin.key_document: Samangjindan-seo (death certificate, requires certified English translation)
origin.source: FCDO South Korea travel advice, updated 2025; British Embassy Seoul guidance
origin.cremation_note: Cremation in South Korea is widely available. Bringing ashes home to the UK is often simpler than full body repatriation. You will need the death certificate, cremation certificate, and export documentation.

destinations.name: United Kingdom
key_documents: Samangjindan-seo (death certificate, certified English translation required)
special_rules: Death is registered at the local government office (Jumin Centre) promptly.
All Korean-language documentation requires a certified English translation for UK acceptance.
The Prosecutor's Office orders post-mortem for suspicious or violent deaths.
An export permit from the relevant Korean authority is required for all repatriations.
destinations.dest_reception: The UK funeral director takes custody at the cargo terminal. All South Korean documentation must be in certified English translation. The coroner for the district is notified. Straightforward cases proceed directly to funeral arrangements.
destinations.dest_consular_note: FCDO 24-hour emergency line: +44 (0)20 7008 5000. The British Embassy in Seoul can register the death and advise on local funeral directors. They cannot pay for or arrange repatriation.
destinations.seo_title: South Korea to UK Repatriation: Family Guide
destinations.seo_description: Someone has died in South Korea. Repatriation to the UK takes 1-2 weeks. Korean death certificate, translation, and export permits all handled. Contact us 24/7.
